mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of months when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the signal fails to produce a negative mean return with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day horizons or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by 100bps annually in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production above 0.5% reflects accelerating manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, triggering positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during early-cycle expansions or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate above 4.0% and a CPIAUCSL index exceeding 205 creates a stagflationary pricing environment that compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in > 50% of periods where both thresholds are met, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms sustained economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative cumulative returns in > 40% of triggered quarters or fails to beat the risk-free rate over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, directly supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 45% of months with payroll gains.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production coinciding with rising federal funds rates indicates a supply-demand mismatch and tightening financial conditions, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.
